description:
GOAL: Add `unique[T]` (or equivalent uniqueness tracking) to Descr so
fz-ul4.19.5's "static path" can identify message values whose
reachability is provably sender-owned at compile time. Currently
fz-ul4.19.5 ships with the dynamic walk-and-decide path only;
that path is always correct but pays an O(reachable-size) walk on
every send.

## Why this ticket is NON-BLOCKING for .19.5

User decision (2026-05-11, option 19.5-c "measure first"):

- fz-ul4.19.5 lands with dynamic path only.
- After .19.5 ships, measure on real programs (or representative
  benchmarks): how often does the dynamic walk fall back to
  COPY vs succeed at TRANSFER? How much wall time does the walk
  itself consume?
- If the dynamic path is "good enough" — most sends transfer, walk
  cost is small — close this ticket without implementing.
- If the dynamic walk is meaningful overhead OR the copy fallback
  is hit often, prioritize implementation.

This ticket exists so the decision is recorded and the option is not
silently lost (per user feedback: every deferral must be approved AND
ticketed).

## What it looks like if we DO implement

### Descr extension (src/types.rs:196-208)

Three options, decide at implementation time:

A. Add a `unique: bool` flag to Descr.
   - Simplest. Union: `a.unique && b.unique` (both must be unique).
     Intersection: `a.unique || b.unique` (either is enough since we're
     narrowing). Top is `unique: false` (conservative).
   - Pollutes every Descr operation with a uniqueness check.

B. Add a parallel `UniqueDescr` wrapper that lives outside Descr.
   - Cleaner separation; Descr unchanged for non-uniqueness analysis.
   - Two parallel lattices to keep in sync.

C. Treat uniqueness as a separate effect/region analysis pass run
   AFTER the main typer.
   - Most decoupled. Closest to linear-types literature.
   - Highest implementation cost; new analysis infrastructure.

Recommend deciding (A) vs (B) vs (C) when this ticket activates,
informed by .19.5 measurement data.

### Propagation rules (sketch)

- Literal allocations (`AllocList`, `AllocStruct`, `MakeClosure`, etc.)
  produce `unique` values.
- Binding a unique value to a Var keeps uniqueness ONLY IF the var has
  exactly one use AND that use consumes the value (e.g., is sent or
  returned). Multiple uses break uniqueness.
- Passing a unique value to a fn that escapes it breaks uniqueness;
  passing to a fn that consumes it (send, return, store-in-unique-
  container) preserves it.
- Reading a field of a unique value: the field is unique only if the
  parent is unique AND the field is not duplicated.

This is essentially affine-type tracking on Descr. Existing literature:
ATS, Rust's borrowck, Clean/Mercury uniqueness types.

### Consumer (fz-ul4.19.5)

At a `send(pid, msg)` site, the typer-inferred Descr of `msg` is
queried. If `Descr::is_unique()` is true, the runtime CAN skip the
walk and transfer the arena directly. The dynamic walk-and-decide path
remains the fallback when Descr is non-unique (or when the typer is
disabled).

## Open questions to answer at activation time

1. How accurate must inference be? If the typer is too conservative
   (under-marks unique), the static path doesn't help. If too aggressive
   (over-marks), the runtime asserts the walk anyway as a safety check
   and we degrade to dynamic. Conservative-by-default is the safe call.
2. Does this require linear/affine syntax in the source language
   (e.g., `unique[Foo]` type annotations)? Probably not for v1 —
   inference-only is fine; explicit annotations are a usability win
   for later.
3. Interaction with closures: a unique value captured into a closure
   becomes shared with the closure's body. Inference must handle this.

## Tests (when activated)

- A literal allocation followed by immediate send: typer marks message
  unique; .19.5 confirms transfer fast path was taken.
- An allocation aliased into two variables before send: typer marks
  non-unique; .19.5 confirms walk fired.
- A nested structure where the outer is unique but contains a shared
  inner: typer marks the outer as non-unique (conservative).
- Closure captures a unique value: subsequent uses of the original
  binding are non-unique.

## Dependencies

- Activation depends on fz-ul4.19.5 measurement data. Currently
  speculative.

## Out of scope

- Source-language uniqueness annotations.
- Borrow-checker-style move tracking on local Vars (related but
  separate; could be a fz-ul4.2x typer feature).

## References

- src/types.rs:196-208 (Descr definition)
- src/ir_typer.rs (typer pass; where uniqueness inference would live)
- fz-ul4.19.5 (the consumer of this analysis)

## Acceptance

- Either: implement uniqueness tracking + wire to .19.5 send path,
  with tests; OR close as "not worth it" after measuring .19.5 in
  practice and documenting the data point.
- One commit (or one close-with-comment) referencing fz-ul4.19.7.
title: unique[T] in Descr — file after measuring .19.5 dynamic path
